area: MSC 39B32/39A06 (functional equations in complex domains, linear difference equations), 30D05 (functional equations for analytic functions), 11B85/11J91 (automatic sequences, Mahler's method) — Mahler-type functional equations and radix-related power series
hole_justification: Two-sided search. (1) Collatz side: located the exact Berg-Meinardus papers and equations via Lagarias' annotated bibliography (arXiv math/0309224, entries 20-21, full text extracted); pulled the complete Semantic Scholar citation list of Berg-Meinardus 1994 (20 citing papers, every one enumerated): it contains Opfer 2011, de Weger's rebuttal context, Berg-Opfer 2013, Neklyudov 2021, Behani 2023 (linear dynamics), Efrem 2025 — and ZERO Mahler-function-theory papers. Grepped full PDFs of Opfer 2011 and Neklyudov: 0 occurrences of 'Mahler'. (2) Mahler side: searched 'Collatz Mahler function rigidity', 'Berg-Meinardus Mahler', 'mixed Mahler equation section operators radix 2 3', 'Collatz automatic set Cobham divergent' — no paper connects them; Adamczewski-Bell (arXiv:1303.2019), Schäfke-Singer (1605.02616), ADH (1910.01874), ADHW (2010.09266) fetched and checked: no Collatz mention. The single near-miss is Bell-Lagarias 2014 (arXiv:1408.6884): Jason Bell — the same Bell as Adamczewski-Bell — wrote on 3x+1 inverse-orbit generating functions, explicitly cites and extends Berg-Meinardus, but uses only the Skolem-Mahler-Lech theorem (unrelated to Mahler functional equations); the rigidity/Galois toolkit is never invoked. Verdict: the BM functional-equation reformulation is fringe (~10 real papers over 30 years); the junction with modern Mahler rigidity/difference-Galois theory is virgin — zero papers.
bridge: EXACT PRIOR OBJECTS. Berg-Meinardus 1994 (Results Math. 25, 1-12): f_m(z)=Σ_n T^m(n) z^n is rational, = p_m(z)/(1−z^{2^m})^2; the bivariate F(z,w)=Σ_{m,n} T^m(n) z^n w^m (|z|<1,|w|<2/3) satisfies F(z^3,w) = z^3/(1−z^3)^2 + wF(z^6,w) + (w/3z)Σ_{j=0}^2 ω^j F(ω^j z^2, w), ω=e^{2πi/3}, and is the unique analytic solution near (0,0). Setting w=1 and dropping the inhomogeneity gives THE equation: h(z^3) = h(z^6) + (1/3z)Σ_{j=0}^2 ω^j h(ω^j z^2). BM Theorem: Collatz ⟺ the only solutions holomorphic in |z|<1 are h = c_0 + c_1·z/(1−z). BM 1995 (Rostock. Math. Kolloq. 48, 11-18): entire solutions are constants; equivalent split system h(z)+h(−z)=2h(z^2) and h(z^3)−h(−z^3)=(2/3z)Σω^j h(ω^j z^2); upper-half-plane form φ(3ξ)+φ(3ξ+3/2)=φ(2ξ)+φ(2ξ+1)+φ(2ξ+2). Opfer 2011 (Hamburger Beiträge HBAM 2011-09) recast these as operators U[h]=h(z)+h(−z)−2h(z^2), V[h]=2H[h]−T_1[h](z)+T_1[h](−z) and claimed ker(U,V)=Δ_2; flaw (author's own June 17 2011 note): the p.11 claim 'the set of all vertices (2n,l) in all levels will contain all even numbers 2n≥6 exactly once' is wrong; de Weger's 2-page note (v0.2, June 9 2011) dissects the gap in Theorem 4.3's annihilation-graph argument. STRUCTURE THEOREM (our normalization, verified coefficientwise): the root-of-unity average is a Cartier/section operator — (1/3)Σ_j ω^j h(ω^j u) = Σ_{n≡2(3)} a_n u^n — and after cancelling the injective substitution z↦z^3, the BM equation is EXACTLY equivalent to the first-order equation h(z) = h(z^2) + z·(Λ_{3,2}h)(z^2), i.e. h ∈ ker L, L := 1 − M_2 − z·M_2·Λ_{3,2}, where M_k h(z)=h(z^k) is the k-Mahler operator and Λ_{3,2}(Σa_n z^n)=Σ a_{3n+2}z^n is the 3-Cartier operator. Coefficientwise this says precisely a_n = a_{T(n)} for all n≥1: solutions = functions constant on weakly-connected components of the Collatz graph, so dim ker L in H(D) = 1 + #components on N≥1, and Collatz ⟺ dim = 2 (basis 1, z/(1−z)); every hypothetical divergent orbit or exotic cycle contributes exactly one extra dimension via its component indicator series h_C = Σ_{n∈C} z^n. THE ANALOGY: pure 2-Mahler equations (polynomial relations among h(z),h(z^2),h(z^4),...) ↔ base-2 self-similar/2-regular sequences; pure 3-Mahler ↔ base-3; the rigidity theorems (Adamczewski-Bell, Schäfke-Singer, ADHW) say the two worlds intersect only in rational functions — the function-field Cobham/Furstenberg-×2×3 phenomenon. The BM operator L sits exactly in the gap: it is ONE mixed equation in the crossed algebra C(z)⟨M_2, Λ_3⟩ (M_2 = even Collatz step, z·M_2·Λ_{3,2} = the odd step (3n+1)/2 read backwards), rather than TWO pure equations in separate bases. Collatz-in-BM-form = 'compute the kernel of the simplest nontrivial mixed (2,3) Mahler-Cartier operator'. Corresponding dictionary: components C ↔ solutions h_C; eventual periodicity ↔ rationality (Fatou/SML for bounded integer coefficients); 2-automatic certificate sets ↔ 2-Mahler solutions (0/1 2-automatic ⟹ 2-regular ⟹ 2-Mahler); Monks 2006 (no semilinear invariant set) ↔ 'no rational exotic solution'; our ≤7-state DFA exhaustion ↔ finite slice of 'no 2-Mahler exotic solution'.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Adamczewski-Bell 2017 (Ann. Sc. Norm. Super. Pisa; arXiv:1303.2019), solving Loxton-van der Poorten's conjecture: for multiplicatively independent k,l ≥ 2, a power series over a characteristic-0 field satisfying both a k-Mahler and an l-Mahler linear functional equation is rational — the Mahler-function Cobham theorem.

Schäfke-Singer 2019 (J. Eur. Math. Soc.; arXiv:1605.02616), 'Consistent systems of linear differential and difference equations': a unified local-analytic/Galois-descent proof and generalization covering pairs of shift, q-difference, and Mahler systems; the technical engine (analysis at common fixed points 0 and 1 of z↦z^2, z↦z^3) most likely to extend to mixed equations.

Adamczewski-Dreyfus-Hardouin-Wibmer (arXiv:2010.09266, JEMS), 'Algebraic independence and linear difference equations': for Mahler operators φ:x↦x^{p1}, σ:x↦x^{p2} with p1,p2 multiplicatively independent, any transcendental solution of a linear φ-equation and any transcendental solution of a linear σ-equation are algebraically independent over C(x) — proved via the σ-Galois theory of linear φ-equations (Ovchinnikov-Wibmer). This settles the strong Loxton-van der Poorten conjecture and is the exact machinery a BM-Galois computation would need.

Adamczewski-Dreyfus-Hardouin 2021 (J. Amer. Math. Soc. 34, 475-503; arXiv:1910.01874): complete hypertranscendence results for solutions of linear Mahler equations — a transcendental Mahler function satisfies no algebraic differential equation (parametrized difference Galois theory).

Dreyfus-Hardouin-Roques 2018 (JEMS; arXiv:1507.03361): hypertranscendence of solutions of order-1 inhomogeneous Mahler equations y(z^p)=a(z)y(z)+b(z) — the exact order-1 shape of our normalized BM equation, minus the Cartier term.

Bell-Lagarias 2014 (arXiv:1408.6884): the 3x+k inverse-orbit generating functions f_{k,m}(z)=Σ_{n→m} z^n have the unit circle as natural boundary for all m except (for k=1) possibly m∈{1,2,4,8}, and Collatz ⟺ f_{1,m} rational for m=1,2,4,8 — root-of-unity singularity-propagation techniques directly reusable for h_C.

Berg-Meinardus 1994/1995 (Results Math. 25, 1-12; Rostock. Math. Kolloq. 48, 11-18): the equivalence theorem itself, plus: the only ENTIRE solutions of the BM equation are constants — a genuine (weak) rigidity theorem already on the books.

Randé-type dichotomy (Bell-Coons-Rowland, J. Integer Seq. 16 (2013); Randé 1992): a k-Mahler function is rational or transcendental, and (Randé) either rational or has the unit circle as natural boundary — consistent with, and explanatory of, Bell-Lagarias.
the_push: Push the analogy through and you get one immediate new theorem and one sharply-posed research program. IMMEDIATE COROLLARY (rigorous today, apparently unrecorded): let C be any component of the Collatz graph other than the {1,2}-component (a divergent-orbit or exotic-cycle certificate). Then h_C(z)=Σ_{n∈C}z^n cannot satisfy both a 2-Mahler and a 3-Mahler equation. Proof: Adamczewski-Bell ⟹ h_C rational; bounded integer coefficients + rational ⟹ C eventually periodic (Fatou/Skolem-Mahler-Lech); Monks 2006 forbids infinite semilinear T-invariant sets avoiding {1,2}. Shadow statement via Cobham: no certificate set is simultaneously 2- and 3-automatic. This strictly extends Monks 2006 (semilinear ⟹ rational ⟹ trivially bi-Mahler) and is the exact functional-equation avatar of Furstenberg ×2×3 disjointness for Collatz. THE REAL PRIZE (one-sided rigidity): kill the second hypothesis using the BM equation itself as the 3-adic structure — Theorem-candidate: 'no exotic solution of L = 1 − M_2 − zM_2Λ_{3,2} is 2-Mahler at all'. Since 0/1 2-regular = 2-automatic ⟹ 2-Mahler, this would rule out EVERY 2-automatic divergence certificate at every state count in one stroke — subsuming and infinitely extending our ≤7-state DFA exhaustion — and would be the analytic Cobham theorem for Collatz. Supporting closure lemma (checked, elementary): if h solves BM and is 2-Mahler, then all twists h(ζz) (ζ^{3^k}=1; note squaring permutes μ_{3^k}, so twists close up into finite M_2-systems) and M_3h are 2-Mahler — because BM rearranges to (1−M_2)(M_3h) = z^{−1}(1/3)Σ_j ω^j M_2(h(ω^j·)), an inhomogeneous order-1 2-Mahler equation with 2-Mahler right side; each M_3-step costs only +1 dimension in the M_2-module tower (but needs μ_{3^{k}}-twists at depth k — the growth of this 'unipotent tower' is the precise technical obstruction). The difference-Galois formulation: a 2-Mahler certificate h_C generates a φ-module (φ=M_2) whose σ-Galois group (σ=M_3, in the Ovchinnikov-Wibmer/ADHW sense) must be small enough to admit the BM relation as a σ-linear constraint; computing/bounding that group and showing genericity forces rationality is exactly an ADHW-style argument one theorem beyond the current literature (ADHW need pure σ-equations for the second function; BM only gives M_3h linearly over the φ-world). HONEST CAVEATS: (i) the Cartier operator Λ_{3,2} is not a field endomorphism, so BM lives outside every existing difference-Galois framework — the missing theory is 'σ-Galois theory of Mahler modules with semilinear sections', genuinely new mathematics; (ii) BM's kernel is infinite-dimensional if Collatz fails and the twist z↦−z already gives ker U infinite dimension, so finiteness assumptions of Picard-Vessiot theory fail globally — one must work with the 2-Mahler filtration, not the full kernel; (iii) ceiling: Conway 1972/Kurtz-Simon 2007 mean the general mixed Mahler-Cartier equation class encodes undecidable iteration problems, so full classification is impossible — only low-complexity fragments (one Cartier term, order 1) can be rigid, and no version of this proves Collatz itself: it excludes certificate classes, exactly like our DFA program but at the top of the hierarchy.
first_move: Two-week concrete plan. LEMMA 0 (paper, 2 pages, do first): write up the normalization L = 1 − M_2 − zM_2Λ_{3,2}, the coefficientwise proof that ker L = {sequences constant on Collatz components} (verified here: a_{2n}=a_n, a_{2m+1}=a_{3m+2} ⟺ a_n = a_{T(n)}), and the Corollary 'no exotic component is both 2-Mahler and 3-Mahler' via Adamczewski-Bell + Fatou + Monks 2006 — this is publishable as a note and stakes the bridge. COMPUTATION (32-core, starts same day): quantitative one-sided rigidity against the 2^71 verification frontier. Any 2-Mahler certificate h with annihilator Σ_{i=0}^r p_i(z)h(z^{2^i})=0, deg p_i ≤ d, has its coefficient sequence determined by an initial segment of length B(r,d) (explicit from the coefficient recurrence once p_0's valuation is controlled; Newton-polygon/valuation lemma — good Lean target); but every component meeting [1, 2^71] is the trivial one, so h agrees with c_0 + c_1 z/(1−z) there, forcing e := h − c_0 − c_1 z/(1−z) to be a 2-Mahler function (order ≤ r+2) vanishing to order > 2^71. Implement the linear-algebra search: for each small (r,d), solve for polynomials p_i annihilating a ker-L element with prescribed component data on n ≤ 10^7 (sparse structured linear systems over Q, embarrassingly parallel over (r,d)); conjecture-then-prove the valuation bound v(r,d) ≤ poly(d)·2^{O(r)}, yielding the theorem 'any 2-Mahler divergence certificate has Mahler complexity (r,d) with v(r,d) > 2^71' — a Hercher-style effective exclusion in the Mahler class that automatically strengthens with every future verification record. GALOIS TRACK (parallel): compute the σ=M_3-Galois group (Ovchinnikov-Wibmer framework, per ADHW) of the order-1 inhomogeneous 2-Mahler equations (1−M_2)g = ψ arising from the closure lemma, starting from the Dreyfus-Hardouin-Roques order-1 classification — the first genuinely new object is the σ-Galois group of the module tower {M_3^k h} with its codim-1 extensions and μ_{3^k}-twists.
payoff_odds: Realistic best case: the one-sided rigidity theorem 'no 2-Mahler (hence no 2-regular/2-automatic of any size) divergence or exotic-cycle certificate exists' — a top-journal Collatz-adjacent result, the analytic Cobham theorem for Collatz, strictly subsuming Monks 2006 and our entire DFA-certificate program; I put 15-30% on this within a serious 1-2 year effort, because it needs a new σ-Galois theory for Mahler modules with Cartier sections (the SS/ADHW toolkit is adjacent but the section operator escapes every existing framework). Likely case (≈85%): the Corollary (no simultaneously (2,3)-Mahler certificate — rigorous today) plus the closure-lemma structure theory plus the quantitative theorem 'no 2-Mahler certificate below explicit complexity v(r,d) > 2^71' — a solid bridge paper connecting the Collatz and Mahler communities, with a growing effective bound tied to verification records. What this route will NOT do: prove Collatz — it excludes certificate classes only, and the Conway/Kurtz-Simon undecidability ceiling guarantees the general mixed Mahler-Cartier theory cannot be fully rigid. Failure mode to watch: the module tower {M_3^k h} may grow twists (μ_{3^k}) fast enough that no finite Galois-theoretic statement closes the loop, leaving only the order-1/regular-singular fragment (still enough for the quantitative theorem via Roques/Faverjon-Poulet algorithms). Net: high-probability moderate payoff, low-probability large payoff, zero-probability jackpot — but the hole is real, cheap to stake, and directly compounds our existing automaton-certificate and 2^71-verification assets.

prior_art.ref: L. Berg, G. Meinardus, Functional equations connected with the Collatz problem, Results in Math. 25 (1994), 1-12. MR 95d:11025
prior_art.what: THE reformulation: f_m rational with denominator (1−z^{2^m})^2; bivariate F(z,w) equation with unique analytic solution; the w=1 homogeneous equation h(z^3)=h(z^6)+(1/3z)Σω^j h(ω^j z^2) whose unit-disk solution space being span{1, z/(1−z)} is EQUIVALENT to Collatz; also Conjecture 2: orbit generating functions g_n(w)=Σ_m T^m(n)w^m rational with denominator 1−w^2.

prior_art.ref: L. Berg, G. Meinardus, The 3n+1 Collatz problem and functional equations, Rostock. Math. Kolloq. 48 (1995), 11-18. MR 97e:11034
prior_art.what: Entire solutions are constants (only existing rigidity theorem for the equation); equivalent split system h(z)+h(−z)=2h(z^2) [i.e. a_{2n}=a_n] and h(z^3)−h(−z^3)=(2/3z)Σω^j h(ω^j z^2); transformation to φ(3ξ)+φ(3ξ+3/2)=φ(2ξ)+φ(2ξ+1)+φ(2ξ+2) on the upper half-plane.

prior_art.ref: G. Opfer, An analytic approach to the Collatz 3n+1 problem, Hamburger Beiträge zur Angewandten Mathematik 2011-09 (2011); submitted to Math. Comp., withdrawn
prior_art.what: Operator recasting U[h]=h(z)+h(−z)−2h(z^2), V[h]; claimed ker(U,V)=Δ_2 ⟹ Collatz; author's own note (June 17, 2011) withdraws the claim: the p.11 'annihilation graph' assertion that all even numbers 2n≥6 appear exactly once is incomplete.

prior_art.ref: B. de Weger, Comments on Opfer's alleged proof of the 3n+1 Conjecture, v0.2, TU Eindhoven, June 9, 2011
prior_art.what: The rebuttal: redefines Opfer's Theorem-4.3 maps j(2l), j''(2l) and shows the annihilation argument assumes exactly what Collatz asserts (weak connectivity of the 3n+1 graph).

prior_art.ref: J. P. Bell, J. C. Lagarias, 3x+1 inverse orbit generating functions almost always have natural boundaries, Acta Arith. (2015); arXiv:1408.6884
prior_art.what: The closest existing bridge (Bell = Adamczewski-Bell's Bell): f_{k,m}(z)=Σ_{n→m}z^n has the unit circle as natural boundary except possibly m∈{1,2,4,8} for k=1; Collatz ⟺ those four are rational. Cites Berg-Meinardus explicitly; uses Skolem-Mahler-Lech, never Mahler functional equations — the rigidity connection is left untouched.

prior_art.ref: B. Adamczewski, J. P. Bell, A problem about Mahler functions, Ann. Sc. Norm. Super. Pisa (2017); arXiv:1303.2019
prior_art.what: (k,l)-rigidity: both k- and l-Mahler (k,l multiplicatively independent) ⟹ rational; solves Loxton-van der Poorten. The theorem the bridge would import.

prior_art.ref: R. Schäfke, M. Singer, Consistent systems of linear differential and difference equations, J. Eur. Math. Soc. 21 (2019); arXiv:1605.02616
prior_art.what: Second proof/generalization via local analysis at common fixed points; the technique most adaptable to mixed equations.

prior_art.ref: B. Adamczewski, T. Dreyfus, C. Hardouin, M. Wibmer, Algebraic independence and linear difference equations, JEMS (2024); arXiv:2010.09266
prior_art.what: Transcendental solutions of a p1-Mahler and a p2-Mahler equation are algebraically independent (σ-Galois theory of φ-equations); the strong rigidity and the exact Galois machinery for the proposed computation.

prior_art.ref: B. Adamczewski, T. Dreyfus, C. Hardouin, Hypertranscendence and linear difference equations, J. Amer. Math. Soc. 34 (2021), 475-503; arXiv:1910.01874
prior_art.what: Transcendental Mahler functions are hypertranscendental; constrains what differential-algebraic structure a certificate h_C could have.

prior_art.ref: T. Dreyfus, C. Hardouin, J. Roques, Hypertranscendence of solutions of Mahler equations, JEMS 20 (2018); arXiv:1507.03361
prior_art.what: Complete order-1 inhomogeneous Mahler theory y(z^p)=a(z)y(z)+b(z) — the shape of the closure-lemma equations (1−M_2)g=ψ.

prior_art.ref: M. Neklyudov, Functional analysis approach to the Collatz conjecture, Results in Math. (2024); arXiv:2106.11859
prior_art.what: Modern operator-theoretic successor of BM: index of Id−T on H^2(D) bounds the number of cycles; hypercyclicity connections. Zero contact with Mahler theory (grep-verified).

prior_art.ref: V. Béhani, Linear dynamics of an operator associated to the Collatz map, arXiv:2303.03203 (2023)
prior_art.what: Hypercyclic/chaotic dynamics of the Collatz transfer operator on weighted Bergman spaces — the linear-dynamics face of ker L; no Mahler/Galois content.

prior_art.ref: C. N. Efrem, Functional equations for generalized Collatz dynamics using integral representations and residue calculus, arXiv:2510.06736 (2025)
prior_art.what: Latest functional-equation derivations (Egorychev method); confirms the line is alive but still never touches Mahler rigidity (checked: no BM-rigidity, no Adamczewski-Bell, no Galois).

prior_art.ref: J. Shallit, D. Wilson, The '3x+1' problem and finite automata (1992); M. Dhiman, R. Pandey, Non-definability of reachability in Büchi arithmetic for a family of generalized Collatz maps, arXiv:2602.06066 (2026)
prior_art.what: The automata shadow of the bridge: finite convergence stages S_i are 2-automatic (S-W); the reachability RELATION is not base-q Büchi-definable via Cobham (D-P). Neither states the 'no k-automatic invariant certificate SET' theorem the Mahler push would deliver.

prior_art.ref: J. Roques, Frobenius method for Mahler equations, arXiv:2503.12995; C. Faverjon, M. Poulet, An algorithm to recognize regular singular Mahler systems, arXiv:2102.10842
prior_art.what: Algorithmic solution-space computation for pure Mahler systems — the computational tools for the (r,d)-exclusion search.
